own_review.issue: Breaking change with no migration path: at base the raw module attribute was passed through and DeferredExtensionContext canonicalized via ModuleIdentifier.fromString, so configs with module="name:main" booted fine. The PR now throws XMLStreamException at parse time for any non-canonical name, so previously-valid standalone.xml/domain.xml configs fail to boot. No auto-correction or relaxed-mode fallback is offered.
Typo in new message id 518: "only if the is slot is not 'main'" — duplicated/misplaced 'is'; should be "only if the slot is not 'main'".

MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR is orphaned — grep over the repo finds zero usages; it is also left non-deprecated while every method it calls is now @Deprecated(forRemoval=true), so it cannot survive the removal. Should be deprecated/removed or moved.

Deprecation cascade: ~12 non-test in-tree files (server deployment module processors, jboss-deployment-structure parsers, elytron ClassLoadingAttributeDefinitions/PermissionMapperDefinitions/DirContextDefinition, domain-http ConsoleMode/ErrorContextHandler) still call the now forRemoval methods, generating build warnings; in-tree callers should be migrated to JBossModulesNameUtil in this PR or a follow-up.

parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier throws raw IllegalArgumentException for malformed names (empty spec, empty slot 'foo:', unterminated escape). In ExtensionAddHandler.execute this escapes as a runtime exception instead of OperationFailedException; in ExtensionXml it bypasses the XMLStreamException-with-location path. Should be caught and converted.
No newline at end of file (also ModuleIdentifierUtil.java); message id 518 says 'Resource /extension=%s' but is also used in the XML-parse context where it is an element, not a resource.

points.point: Typo in id 518 'only if the is slot'
Deprecation cascade: ~14 in-tree callers of forRemoval methods will see warnings
Existing configs with non-canonical names will fail on boot via the ExtensionXml check — intentional breaking change worth confirming
TODO comment on escape-sequence tests inherited from original behavior, fine to leave

missed: IllegalArgumentException from parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier escapes ExtensionAddHandler/ExtensionXml without conversion to OperationFailedException/XMLStreamException
exceeds: Verified that ModuleLoader.loadModule(String) and Module.loadServiceFromCallerModuleLoader(String, Class) exist in jboss-modules 2.1.6.Final (confirmed correct)

points.point: Typo in id 518 'only if the is slot'

ExtensionsLoader (CLI) canonicalizes while DeferredExtensionContext/TransformerRegistry/ApplyExtensionsHandler pass through — asks if defensive for legacy servers; accurate inconsistency observation (CLI can connect to older servers whose model may hold name:main)
ExtensionAddHandler validation is a breaking change for clients/scripts using explicit :main; document for 28.0.0
MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR still works via delegation; 'verify callers are aware' — there are zero callers, so no-action point resting on a wrong premise, but no defect fabricated

missed: The critical boot-path framing: ExtensionXml now rejects previously-bootable configs at XML parse time with no migration path (v7 only flagged the management-API add-time aspect)
MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR is orphaned (zero usages) — v7 looked at it but assumed callers exist
Deprecation cascade across ~12 in-tree non-test callers of the forRemoval methods
exceeds: ExtensionsLoader canonicalization-inconsistency question is beyond both the human review and the judge's own list

qualitative: Both candidates are fabrication-free and caught the id-518 typo; the gap is depth on the one issue that matters. v5 explicitly identified that existing configurations with non-canonical module names will fail at boot when ExtensionXml applies the new check (+2 critical), and also landed the orphaned MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR and the deprecation cascade, missing only the uncaught IllegalArgumentException path. v7 flagged the breaking change only at the management-API/ExtensionAddHandler level and never connected it to boot-time XML parsing of existing configs, missed the cascade entirely, and discussed MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR while assuming it has callers when it has none. v7's one distinctive contribution — the canonicalization inconsistency between the CLI ExtensionsLoader and the server-side pass-through paths — is accurate and genuinely exceeds both references. Human reference points (message hint, moving the util to controller-client) were already incorporated into the final diff, so neither candidate is penalized for them; net result v5 4 vs v7 -1.
